schools.summary: The making of the ark: 'and Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half was the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it' — the making — 'Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood': Bezaleel (the chief workman, Exodus 35:30) making the ark first, the holiest piece, of acacia (shittim) wood overlaid with gold (vv.2, the witnesses noting the significance — the ark the chief and holiest of all the furniture, the throne of God, the chest holding the law, the place of his presence above the mercy seat, Exodus 25:10-22, the order, the ark made first, the most sacred object, the heart of the tabernacle, the materials, the incorruptible wood overlaid with gold, the figure of the humanity and deity of Christ joined, the man Christ Jesus in whom dwelt the fulness of the Godhead, Colossians 2:9, the law within, the ark holding the tables of the law, the figure of Christ in whose heart was the law, Psalm 40:8, 'thy law is within my heart'; Hebrews 10:7, the ark a figure of Christ, the meeting-place of God and man); Bezaleel making the ark according to the pattern (Exodus 25:10, the exact dimensions, two cubits and a half by a cubit and a half) — the faithful work (the workman making all according to the pattern shown in the mount, Exodus 25:40; Hebrews 8:5, the obedience, the work exactly as God commanded); the ark of shittim wood — the holiest furniture, the throne of God (the providence, the ark made first, the figure of Christ the meeting-place of God and man, the law within, the gold and wood) — Calvin, Wesley, Henry, Gill.

sides.position: The throne of God and the chest of the law (Exodus 25:10-22), made first as the holiest furniture - the incorruptible wood overlaid with gold a figure of the deity and humanity of Christ joined (Colossians 2:9), the law within him (Psalm 40:8; Hebrews 10:7), the ark the meeting-place of God and man, a figure of Christ

themes: And Bezaleel made the ark of shittim wood: two cubits and a half was the length of it, and a cubit and a half the breadth of it, and a cubit and a half the height of it
the making of the ark (the crux of the throne of God and the figure of Christ)
the holiest furniture, the throne of God and the chest of the law (Exodus 25:10-22)
the figure of Christ in whom God and man meet, the law within his heart (Colossians 2:9; Psalm 40:8)

schools.summary: The ark overlaid with gold within and without: 'and he overlaid it with pure gold within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about' — the ark overlaid with pure gold inside and out, with a golden crown (rim, molding) around the top (the gold within and without, the ark gold inside and out, wholly pure, the crown, the golden rim, the ornament and the holding-rim for the mercy seat, the figure, the gold within and without, the figure of Christ pure within and without, wholly holy, or the believer adorned within and without); the gold within and without (the ark wholly overlaid, pure inside and out, Exodus 25:11) — the figure (the inner and outer purity, the wood hidden by the gold, the figure of Christ glorious within and without, or grace within answering to the profession without); 'made a crown of gold to it round about' (the golden crown, the rim, the ornament around the top, Exodus 25:11, the figure, the crown, the kingly glory, the ark crowned, a figure of Christ crowned with glory and honour, Hebrews 2:9, the providence, the ark overlaid and crowned, the figure of Christ glorious and pure) — Keil and Delitzsch.

themes: And he overlaid it with pure gold within and without, and made a crown of gold to it round about
the ark overlaid with gold within and without
the ark wholly pure, gold inside and out (Exodus 25:11)
the crown, a figure of Christ crowned with glory and honour (Hebrews 2:9)

schools.summary: The staves put in to bear the ark: 'and he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, to bear the ark' — the staves (poles) put into the rings at the sides of the ark, to carry it (the staves, the poles for bearing the ark, never to be removed, Exodus 25:15, the bearing, the ark carried by the staves, borne on the shoulders of the Levites, Numbers 7:9, the figure, the ark ever ready to be borne, the presence of God moving with his people through the wilderness); the staves in the rings (the poles for carrying, Exodus 25:14-15) — the readiness (the ark ever ready to journey, the staves not removed, the figure, God's presence going with his pilgrim people); 'to bear the ark' (the ark borne by the staves, carried on the shoulders, Numbers 7:9, the figure, the presence of God moving with his people, the ark going before them, Numbers 10:33-36, the providence, the ark ever ready to be borne, God's presence with his pilgrim people) — JFB.

themes: And he put the staves into the rings by the sides of the ark, to bear the ark
the staves put in to bear the ark
the ark ever ready to be borne, the staves not removed (Exodus 25:15)
the presence of God moving with his pilgrim people (Numbers 7:9; 10:33-36)

schools.summary: The making of the mercy seat: 'and he made the mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half was the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof' — the mercy seat (the propitiatory, the golden lid of the ark) made of pure gold, the same length and breadth as the ark (the mercy seat, the cover of the ark, the place of atonement and meeting, Exodus 25:17-22, the pure gold, wholly of gold, no wood, unlike the ark, the propitiatory, the place where God met his people in mercy, above the law, over the blood-sprinkled lid); the mercy seat of pure gold (the propitiatory, Exodus 25:17) — the figure (the mercy seat, the Greek 'hilasterion,' the propitiation, Christ 'set forth to be a propitiation,' Romans 3:25, the place where mercy covers the broken law, Christ our mercy seat, 1 John 2:2; Hebrews 9:5); the mercy seat the same size as the ark (the exact cover, Exodus 25:17) — the lid fitting the ark (mercy covering the law, the providence, the mercy seat, the place of atonement, the figure of Christ the propitiation) — Clarke.

themes: And he made the mercy seat of pure gold: two cubits and a half was the length thereof, and one cubit and a half the breadth thereof
the making of the mercy seat
the place of atonement and meeting, mercy covering the broken law (Exodus 25:17-22)
the figure of Christ the propitiation (Romans 3:25; 1 John 2:2; Hebrews 9:5)

schools.summary: The making of the cherubim on the mercy seat: 'and he made two cherubims of gold, beaten out of one piece made he them, on the two ends of the mercy seat' — the two cherubim (angelic figures) made of gold, beaten out of one piece with the mercy seat, on its two ends (the cherubim, the angelic figures overshadowing the mercy seat, Exodus 25:18-20, the one piece, the cherubim and mercy seat of one beaten work, undivided, the figure, the cherubim, the angels desiring to look into the things of redemption, 1 Peter 1:12, the angels attending the throne of grace); the cherubim of beaten gold, one piece with the mercy seat (Exodus 25:18) — the figure (the angelic guardians of the throne, the cherubim of glory, Hebrews 9:5, the angels attending the place of atonement); 'on the two ends of the mercy seat' (the cherubim on the ends, facing each other and the mercy seat, Exodus 25:20, the figure, the angels looking down on the place of atonement, the mystery of redemption into which they desire to look, 1 Peter 1:12, the providence, the cherubim overshadowing the mercy seat, the angels attending the throne of grace) — Ellicott.

themes: And he made two cherubims of gold, beaten out of one piece made he them, on the two ends of the mercy seat
the making of the cherubim on the mercy seat
the angelic guardians overshadowing the throne of grace (Exodus 25:18-20; Hebrews 9:5)
the angels desiring to look into the things of redemption (1 Peter 1:12)

schools.summary: The making of the table of shewbread: 'and he made the table of shittim wood: two cubits was the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof' — the making — 'he made the table of shittim wood': the table of shewbread (the bread of the Presence) made of acacia wood overlaid with gold (vv.11), set in the holy place (the witnesses noting the significance — the table holding the twelve loaves of shewbread, the bread of the Presence, the twelve tribes before God continually, Exodus 25:23-30; Leviticus 24:5-9, the figure — the table of the LORD, the bread of the Presence, a figure of Christ the bread of life, John 6:35, 'I am the bread of life,' the food of his people, and the communion, the table of the LORD, the believer's fellowship and feeding upon Christ, 1 Corinthians 10:16-21, 'the table of the Lord,' the bread ever before God, the tribes represented continually before him, the people always in his presence); the table of shewbread (the bread continually before God, the provision of God's house) — the figure (Christ the bread, the food of the soul, the table spread for God's people, the continual provision, the lesson, the soul fed upon Christ, the bread of the Presence); the table of acacia overlaid with gold — the table of the Presence (the providence, the table holding the bread, the figure of Christ the bread of life and the communion of his people) — Wesley, Henry, Clarke.

debates.question: What does the table of shewbread signify?

sides.position: The table of the Presence holding the twelve loaves, the tribes ever before God (Exodus 25:23-30; Leviticus 24:5-9) - a figure of Christ the bread of life (John 6:35) and the communion of his people at the table of the LORD (1 Corinthians 10:16-21)

themes: And he made the table of shittim wood: two cubits was the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof, and a cubit and a half the height thereof
the making of the table of shewbread (the crux of the table of the Presence)
the table holding the bread of the Presence, the tribes ever before God (Exodus 25:23-30; Leviticus 24:5-9)
a figure of Christ the bread of life and the communion of his people (John 6:35; 1 Corinthians 10:16-21)

schools.summary: The making of the vessels of the table: 'and he made the vessels which were upon the table, his dishes, and his spoons, and his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, of pure gold' — the vessels of the table (dishes, spoons, bowls, and covers) made of pure gold (the vessels, the furniture for the shewbread and the drink offerings, Exodus 25:29, the pure gold, all the vessels of gold, the holiness of the service, the figure, the vessels of the sanctuary, the holy things, the vessels of honour, 2 Timothy 2:20-21); the dishes and spoons (the vessels for the bread, the various uses, Exodus 25:29) — the service (the furniture for the table of the Presence); 'his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, of pure gold' (the bowls and covers, for the drink offerings and the covering of the bread, the pure gold, all of gold, the holiness, the providence, the vessels of the table, all of pure gold, the holy furniture of God's house) — Clarke, Ellicott.

themes: And he made the vessels which were upon the table, his dishes, and his spoons, and his bowls, and his covers to cover withal, of pure gold
the making of the vessels of the table
the furniture for the shewbread and the drink offerings, all of pure gold (Exodus 25:29)
the holy vessels of the sanctuary, the vessels of honour (2 Timothy 2:20-21)

schools.summary: The making of the candlestick: 'and he made the candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work made he the candlestick; his shaft, and his branch, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, were of the same' — the making — 'he made the candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work': the golden candlestick (lampstand) made of pure gold, of beaten work (hammered out of one talent of gold), with its shaft, branches, bowls, knops (knobs), and flowers (Exodus 25:31-40, the witnesses noting the significance — the candlestick the light of the holy place, the seven-branched lampstand, the only light, burning continually, the figure — the light of divine revelation, the light of the Spirit, and the church the light-bearer, the candlestick, or the seven lamps, a figure of the church holding forth the light, Revelation 1:20, the seven candlesticks the seven churches, the church the light of the world, Matthew 5:14-16, the light fed by the oil of the Spirit, Zechariah 4:2-6, 'not by might, nor by power, but by my spirit,' the beaten work, the candlestick beaten, formed by blows, the figure of the church or Christ formed through suffering, the one piece, the shaft and branches of one beaten work, the unity of the light); the candlestick of beaten gold (the light of the sanctuary, the only light in the holy place) — the figure (Christ the light, John 8:12, or the church the light-bearer, holding forth the word of life, Philippians 2:15-16, the continual light, the lamps ever burning, the light of testimony); the ornaments all of one beaten work (the unity, Exodus 25:31) — the providence (the candlestick of one piece, the light of the sanctuary, the figure of the Spirit's light and the church) — Wesley, JFB, Clarke.

sides.position: The light of the holy place, the seven-branched lampstand of beaten gold burning continually (Exodus 25:31-40) - a figure of the light of divine revelation and the Spirit, the church the light-bearer (Revelation 1:20; Matthew 5:14-16), the light fed by the oil of the Spirit (Zechariah 4:2-6)

themes: And he made the candlestick of pure gold: of beaten work made he the candlestick; his shaft, and his branch, his bowls, his knops, and his flowers, were of the same
the making of the candlestick (the crux of the light of the sanctuary)
the light of the holy place, the seven-branched lampstand burning continually (Exodus 25:31-40)
a figure of the light of revelation and the Spirit, the church the light-bearer (Revelation 1:20; Zechariah 4:2-6)

schools.summary: The almond-shaped ornaments of the candlestick: 'three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch, a knop and a flower; and three bowls made like almonds in another branch, a knop and a flower: so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick' — each of the six branches having three bowls shaped like almonds, with a knop (knob) and a flower (the almond design, the bowls fashioned like almond blossoms, the candlestick formed like a flowering almond tree, the six branches, the branches going out of the central shaft, the figure, the almond, the tree that wakes first, the figure of watchfulness and life, as Aaron's rod that budded almonds, Numbers 17:8, the light and life of God's house); the almond-shaped bowls (the candlestick like a flowering almond, Exodus 25:33) — the figure (the almond, the waker, the figure of life and watchfulness, Jeremiah 1:11-12); 'so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick' (the six branches, three on each side, with the central shaft, seven lights, the figure, the one candlestick with seven lights, the fulness of the Spirit's light, Revelation 4:5, the providence, the candlestick like a flowering tree, the light and life of God's house) — Ellicott.

themes: Three bowls made after the fashion of almonds in one branch, a knop and a flower... so throughout the six branches going out of the candlestick
the almond-shaped ornaments of the candlestick
the candlestick formed like a flowering almond tree (Exodus 25:33; Numbers 17:8)
the figure of life and the sevenfold light of the Spirit (Revelation 4:5)

schools.summary: The seven lamps of the candlestick: 'and he made his seven lamps, and his snuffers, and his snuffdishes, of pure gold' — the seven lamps of the candlestick, with the snuffers and snuffdishes (for tending the wicks), made of pure gold (the seven lamps, the seven lights of the candlestick, Exodus 25:37, the snuffers and snuffdishes, the instruments for trimming the lamps, the figure, the seven lamps, the seven Spirits of God, the fulness of the Spirit, Revelation 4:5, the light tended and kept burning); the seven lamps (the seven lights, the fulness, Exodus 25:37) — the figure (the seven Spirits of God, the perfect light, Revelation 1:4; 4:5; Zechariah 4:2); 'his snuffers, and his snuffdishes, of pure gold' (the instruments for tending the lamps, the trimming of the wicks, the light kept bright, the figure, the light tended and kept burning, the church's light to be trimmed, Matthew 25:7, the providence, the seven lamps and their instruments, the light of the sanctuary kept burning) — JFB.

themes: And he made his seven lamps, and his snuffers, and his snuffdishes, of pure gold
the seven lamps of the candlestick
the seven lights of the sanctuary, the figure of the fulness of the Spirit (Exodus 25:37; Revelation 4:5)
the light tended and kept burning (Matthew 25:7)

schools.summary: The making of the altar of incense: 'and he made the incense altar of shittim wood: the length of it was a cubit, and the breadth of it a cubit; it was foursquare; and two cubits was the height of it; the horns thereof were of the same' — the golden altar of incense made of acacia wood (overlaid with gold, vv.26), a cubit square and two cubits high, with horns of one piece (the altar of incense, the golden altar, in the holy place before the veil, Exodus 30:1-10, the foursquare, the altar square, the figure of stability and the four corners of the earth, the horns, the horns of the altar, the points of power and refuge); the altar of incense (the place where the incense was burned, the figure of prayer) — the figure (the incense the prayers of the saints, Psalm 141:2, 'let my prayer be set forth before thee as incense'; Revelation 8:3-4, the incense offered with the prayers of the saints, the golden altar of intercession, a figure of Christ's intercession, Hebrews 7:25, the continual incense, the prayer ascending continually); 'the horns thereof were of the same' (the horns of one piece with the altar, Exodus 30:2, the figure, the horns of the altar, the power and the place of refuge, 1 Kings 1:50, the blood of atonement on the horns, Exodus 30:10, the providence, the altar of incense, the figure of prayer and Christ's intercession) — Henry, Clarke, Wesley.

themes: And he made the incense altar of shittim wood: the length of it was a cubit, and the breadth of it a cubit; it was foursquare; and two cubits was the height of it; the horns thereof were of the same
the making of the altar of incense
the golden altar before the veil, the figure of prayer (Exodus 30:1-10; Psalm 141:2; Revelation 8:3-4)
a figure of Christ's continual intercession (Hebrews 7:25)

schools.summary: The making of the holy anointing oil and the pure incense: 'and he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of sweet spices, according to the work of the apothecary' — the making — 'he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of sweet spices, according to the work of the apothecary': the holy anointing oil (of choice spices, Exodus 30:22-33) and the pure sweet incense (Exodus 30:34-38) made by the perfumer's art (the witnesses noting the significance — the anointing oil for consecrating the tabernacle, its furniture, and the priests, the holy oil, not to be imitated or put on common flesh, Exodus 30:31-33, the figure — the anointing oil a figure of the Holy Spirit, the unction, the anointing of the Spirit, 'ye have an unction from the Holy One,' 1 John 2:20, 27, the Spirit's anointing of Christ, the Anointed, and of his people, 2 Corinthians 1:21-22; Acts 10:38, the incense a figure of prayer, the prayers of the saints, Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8; 8:3-4, and of the merit and intercession of Christ that perfumes the prayers of his people, the holy oil and incense, set apart, holy, not to be counterfeited, the Spirit and true prayer not to be imitated by the flesh); the pure incense made by the apothecary (the perfumer, the sweet spices compounded, Exodus 30:34) — the figure (the incense of prayer, the sweet odour, the prayers of the saints perfumed by Christ's merit, Revelation 8:3-4; Ephesians 5:2, Christ's sacrifice 'a sweetsmelling savour'); the anointing oil and the incense — figures of the Spirit's anointing and the prayers of the saints (the providence, the holy oil and incense, set apart for God's service, figures of the Spirit and prayer, the chapter closing the making of the holy things) — Clarke, Ellicott.

debates.question: What do the holy anointing oil and the pure incense signify?

sides.position: The oil a figure of the Holy Spirit's anointing (1 John 2:20, 27; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22), and the incense a figure of prayer (Psalm 141:2; Revelation 5:8; 8:3-4) perfumed by Christ's merit - the holy things set apart, not to be counterfeited (Exodus 30:31-38)

themes: And he made the holy anointing oil, and the pure incense of sweet spices, according to the work of the apothecary
the making of the holy anointing oil and the pure incense (the crux of the Spirit's anointing and prayer)
the anointing oil a figure of the Holy Spirit's unction (1 John 2:20, 27; 2 Corinthians 1:21-22)
the incense a figure of the prayers of the saints, perfumed by Christ's merit (Psalm 141:2; Revelation 8:3-4)
